questions.questionKo: 귀사의 글로벌 총 Scope 1 배출량을 시설(facility) 단위로 구분하여 입력하십시오.
questions.reviewStatus: 변경 없음 확인 및 2026 원문 반영
questions.searchText: 7.17.2 break down your total gross global scope 1 emissions by business facility. environmental performance - climate change matrix oil & gas, coal, general, aviation, capital goods, transport oems - epm, chemicals, metals & mining, transport services, steel, agricultural commodities, transport oems, energy utilities & power generators, cement, food, beverage & tobacco, paper & forestry, real estate, construction 사업장 별로 총 글로벌 scope 1 배출량을 세분화하십시오 . ` m7. environmental performance - climate change 사업장 scope 1 배출량 (co2e 미터톤 ) 위도 경도 사업부문별을 최대 500 자로 작성 숫자 입력 소수점 최대 3 자리 입력 no change - 2026 source verified 7.17.2.1 facility rich_text 7.17.2.2 scope 1 emissions (metric tons co2e) numeric 7.17.2.3 latitude numeric 7.17.2.4 longitude numeric change from last year no change question dependencies question dependencies this question only appears if you select "by facility" in response to 7.17. requested content general cdp provides a place for companies to provide basic data for the geo-location of their facilities. this information will be useful to link cdp data with other sources of information and can help investors assess physical risks of climate change and exposure of assets. it will also help cdp to link the information requested by investors to cities preparing their inventory for cdp. if your organization has scope 1 emissions from non-stationary sources (i.e. transportation vehicles) that... additional information latitude and longitude: latitude and longitude are geographic coordinates that specify, respectively, the north-south and east-west position, of a point on the earth's surface. they are expressed as angular measures and thus, latitude can vary from +90° to -90° and longitude from +180° to -180°. the geodetic system that should be used is the wgs 84, which is the system used by gps (global positioning system), google maps, google earth, and all major web applications providing coordinates to u... 사업장 별로 총 글로벌 scope 1 배출량을 세분화하십시오 . ` 환경 성과 - epm, chemicals, metals & mining, transport services, steel, agricultural commodities, transport oems, energy utilities & power generators, cement, food, beverage & tobacco, paper & forestry, real estate, construction 사업 활동별로 총 글로벌 scope 1 배출량을 세분화하십시오 . ` m7. environmental performance - climate change 사업 활동 scope 1 배출량 (co2e 미터톤 ) 사업 부문별을 최대 500 자로 작성 숫자 입력 소수점 최대 3 자리 입력 no change - 2026 source verified 7.17.3.1 activity rich_text 7.17.3.2 scope 1 emissions (metric tons co2e) numeric change from last year no change question dependencies question dependencies this question only appears if you select "by activity" in response to 7.17. requested content activity (column 1) using no more than 500 characters, state the activity you are disclosing scope 1 emissions for. for more details on which activities to report, see guidance to 7.17. scope 1 emissions (metric tons co2e) (column 2) report your organization's greenhouse gas emissions in co2-equivalent for the activity stated in column 1. negative numbers are not allowed as organizations are to report gross, not net figures. emissions figures should be for the reporting year only (as defined ... 사업 활동별로 총 글로벌 scope 1 배출량을 세분화하십시오 . ` 환경 성과 - additional guidance added guidance regarding the publication of land sector and removals standard. change from last year additional guidance question dependencies this question only appears if you select "own land only" or "value chain (including own land)" in response to column "relevance of emissions and/or water-related impacts" for the "production" row of 1.11, or you select "direct operations" or "both direct operations and upstream/downstream value chain" in response to column " relevance of emissions and/or water-related impacts" for the "processing/manufacturing" or "distribution" rows of 1.11. requested content general if your organization has calculated emissions from your relevant business activities (i.e. agricultural/forestry, processing/manufacturing and/or distribution) and these emissions are included in the global gross scope 1 emissions figure reported in 7.6, please select "yes", if these emissions have been included in their entirety, or "partially", if some of these emissions were included. otherwise, select "no". the ghg protocol has developed new land sector and removals standard (lsrs... 귀사는 include emissions pertaining to your business activity(ies) in your 직접 운영사업장 as part of your global gross scope 1 figure입니까? questions.searchText: 7.18.1 select the form(s) in which you are reporting your agricultural/forestry emissions. environmental performance - climate change single_select agricultural commodities, food, beverage & tobacco, paper & forestry [국문 번역 검토 필요] select the form(s) in which you are reporting your agricultural/forestry emissions. modified guidance updated guidance regarding the publication of land sector and removals standard. change from last year modified guidance question dependencies this question only appears if you select "yes" or "partially" in response to 7.18, and select "own land only" or "value chain (including own land)" in response to the "production" row in 1.11 column "relevance of emissions and/or water-related impacts". requested content general note that the ghg protocol agricultural guidance recommends that scope 1 emissions should be disaggregated by the following categories: non-mechanical: emissions from biological processes shaped by climatic and soil conditions or the burning of crop/timber residues. land use change: emissions from land use change that results in a reduction in the size of carbon stocks e.g. from the conversion of native habitats into farmlands/production units. mechanical: emissions from equipment or ... the form(s) in which you are reporting your agricultural/forestry emissions.을/를 선택하십시오. 환경 성과 - protocol has developed new land sector and removals standard (lsrs) and guidance . due to the timeline of publication, organizations are not yet able to report emissions and removals in line with the lsrs in cdp's 2026 corporate questionnaire. organizations responding to this question during the 2026 disclosure cycle should report ... additional information refer to: the ghg protocol agricultural guidance for further details on responding to this question and; the ghg protocol corporate accounting and reporting standards for general guidance on standards and calculation tools. example response for a company disclosing total agricultural emissions: activity emissions (metric tons co 2 e) methodology please explain agriculture/forestry 200 region-specific emissions factors; field measurements questions.questionText: Why do you not include greenhouse gas emissions pertaining your business activity(ies) in your direct operations as part of your global gross Scope 1 figure? Describe any plans to do so in the future.

climate change 사업 부문 scope 2 위치 기반 배출량 (co2e 미터톤 ) scope 2 시장 기반 배출량 (co2e 미터톤 ) 사업 부문별을 최대 500 자로 작성 숫자 입력 소수점 최대 3 자리 입력 숫자 입력 소수점 최대 3 자리 입력 no change - 2026 source verified 7.20.1.1 business division rich_text 7.20.1.2 scope 2, location-based (metric tons co2e) numeric 7.20.1.3 scope 2, market-based (metric tons co2e) numeric change from last year no change question dependencies this question only appears if you select "business division" in response to 7.20. requested content business division (column 1) using no more than 500 characters, state the business division you are disclosing scope 2 emissions for. scope 2, location-based (metric tons co2e) (column 2) report your organization's scope 2 emissions in co2e for the business division stated in column 1, on a location-based method, i.e. reflecting the average emissions intensity of grids on which energy consumption occurs. negative numbers are not allowed as organizations are to report gross, not net figures. e... 사업 부문별로 총 글로벌 scope 2 배출량을 세분화하십시오 . ` 환경 성과 - questions.questionKo: 귀사의 글로벌 총 Scope 2 배출량을 시설(facility) 단위로 구분하여 입력하십시오.
questions.searchText: 7.20.2 break down your total gross global scope 2 emissions by business facility. environmental performance - climate change matrix oil & gas, coal, general, aviation, capital goods, transport oems - epm, chemicals, metals & mining, transport services, steel, agricultural commodities, transport oems, cement, food, beverage & tobacco, paper & forestry, real estate, construction 사업장별로 총 글로벌 scope 2 배출량을 세분화하십시오 . ` m7. environmental performance - climate change 사업장 scope 2 위치 기반 배출량 (co2e 미터톤 ) scope 2 시장 기반 배출량 (co2e 미터톤 ) 사업 부문별을 최대 500 자로 작성 숫자 입력 소수점 최대 3 자리 입력 숫자 입력 소수점 최대 3 자리 입력 no change - 2026 source verified 7.20.2.1 facility rich_text 7.20.2.2 scope 2, location-based (metric tons co2e) numeric 7.20.2.3 scope 2, market-based (metric tons co2e) numeric change from last year no change question dependencies this question only appears if you select "by facility" in response to 7.20. requested content facility (column 1) using no more than 500 characters, identify the facility you are disclosing scope 1 emissions for. if your organization has scope 2 emissions from non-stationary sources that cannot be attributed to a specific facility then you can report the emissions from these sources collectively in one row. you can identify these emissions by inputting "non-stationary sources" in this column. scope 2, location-based (metric tons co2e) (column 2) report your organization's scope 2 emis... 사업장별로 총 글로벌 scope 2 배출량을 세분화하십시오 . ` 환경 성과 - questions.questionText: Break down your gross Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions between your consolidated accounting group and other entities included in your response.
questions.questionKo: 귀사의 총 Scope 1 및 Scope 2 배출량을 회계상 연결실체(종속기업/자회사 등)과 응답에 포함한 기타 피투자자로 구분하여 입력하십시오.
questions.reviewStatus: 변경 없음 확인 및 2026 원문 반영
questions.searchText: 7.22 break down your gross scope 1 and scope 2 emissions between your consolidated accounting group and other entities included in your response. environmental performance - climate change matrix oil & gas, coal, general, aviation, capital goods, transport oems - epm, chemicals, metals & mining, transport services, steel, agricultural commodities, transport oems, energy utilities & power generators, cement, food, beverage & tobacco, paper & forestry, real estate, construction 귀사의 총 scope 1 및 scope 2 배출량을 회계상 연결실체(종속기업/자회사 등)과 응답에 포함한 기타 피투자자로 구분하여 입력하십시오. no change - 2026 source verified 7.22.1 scope 1 emissions (metric tons co2e) numeric 7.22.2 scope 2, location-based emissions (metric tons co2e) numeric 7.22.3 scope 2, market-based emissions (metric tons co2e) numeric 7.22.4 please explain rich_text change from last year no change requested content general the "consolidated accounting group" refers to the group of entities for which information is included within your annual financial statements. for example, for an organization applying ifrs accounting standards, this group would comprise the parent organization and its consolidated subsidiaries. "all other entities" refers to any entities for which you have included emissions data for in 7.6 and 7.7, but do not fall within the consolidated accounting group. for example, for an entity ... ambition companies are transparent about their emissions inventories, including the emissions associated with the consolidated accounting group. 연결 회계 그룹과 응답에 포함된 다른 법인 간의 총 scope 1 및 scope 2 배출량을 세분화하십시오 . ` 환경 성과 - ` m7. environmental performance - climate change 예 아니오 자회사가 없기 때문에 관련 없음 modified guidance clarification of guidance around consoldiation approach & reporting boundary. change from last year modified guidance requested content general the "consolidation approach" (reported in 6.1) identifies which entities are included within the boundary that you are using for your disclosure of climate-related data (i.e. financial control, operational control, equity share or other). select "yes" if your responses to questions 7.6, 7.7 and 7.8 include emissions data from subsidiaries, based on the approach selected in 6.1, and you are able to provide a breakdown of these emissions by subsidiary. if you select "yes" you will be as... ambition companies are transparent about their emissions inventories, including their subsidiaries. explanation of terms explanation of terms subsidiary: a company owned or controlled by a parent company or holding company. consolidation approach: the identification of entities (companies, businesses, other groups, etc.) relevant to the environmental impact of the responding organization. the ghg protocol states that two distinct approaches may be used to consolidate ghg emissions; the equity share and the control approaches. control can be defined in either financial (financial control) or operational (operati... 귀사는 cdp 응답에 포함된 자회사의 배출량 데이터를 세분화할 수 있습니까 ? requested content general the values to be reported are the total combined gross scope 1 methane emissions determined for the given business division(s) (including vents, leaks, etc.,) expressed as a percentage of the aggregated production or throughput of natural gas and total hydrocarbons, respectively, at the given business divisions. if you select "other, please specify," provide a label for the oil and gas business division. estimated total methane emitted expressed as % of natural gas production or throu... additional information methane emissions from the oil and gas sector: methane metrics, which include emissions rates, will make data more actionable and aid in the assessment of methane performance. emission rate refers to the percentage of total methane volume which is being lost as a function of production or throughput. the latest scientific studies on methane emissions from the natural gas and oil industries suggest that, in order to maximize the climate benefits of a transition from both diesel and coal to nat... explanation of terms estimated/modelled data: data based on bottom-up calculation approaches, such as multiplying activity data by standardized emission factors. observational data: data based on top-down methods, such as using airborne or satellite sensors. report your methane emissions as percentages of natural gas 및 hydrocarbon production 또는 questions.searchText: 7.29 what percentage of your total operational spend in the reporting year was on energy? environmental performance - climate change single_select financial services, oil & gas, coal, general, aviation, capital goods, transport oems - epm, chemicals, metals & mining, transport services, steel, agricultural commodities, transport oems, energy utilities & power generators, cement, food, beverage & tobacco, paper & forestry, real estate, construction 보고연도 내, 귀사의 총 운영비에서 에너지 비용이 차지하는 비중(%)을 입력하십시오. no change - 2026 source verified change from last year no change requested content general ensure that the boundary used for calculating your operational spend is the same as that for your energy spend (i.e. it includes the same facilities, geographies, etc.). "operational spend" should exclude extraordinary expenses such as gains or losses on the sale of assets. the calculation should also exclude the cost of interest or taxes on profits. explanation of terms operational spend: operational spend should be the sum of the costs for the following two types of costs to the business: - cost of goods sold (also known as 'direct costs'): this generally refers to the raw material, energy and labor costs directly identified in the cost of the end product. these costs fluctuate and vary depending on the number or volume of goods sold. - operating costs (also known as 'indirect costs' or 'overheads'): this generally refers to the essential expenses incurred ... 보고 연도의 총 운영 지출 중 에너지에 대한 지출은 몇 퍼센트였습니까 ? ` 환경 성과 - 기후변화 단일 선택 climate change 금융 서비스, 석유 및 가스, 석탄, 일반, 항공, 자본재, 운송 oem - questions.questionText: Select which energy-related activities your organization has undertaken.
questions.questionKo: 귀사에 해당하는 에너지 관련 활동을 선택하십시오.
questions.reviewStatus: 변경 없음 확인 및 2026 원문 반영
questions.searchText: 7.30 select which energy-related activities your organization has undertaken. environmental performance - climate change matrix financial services, oil & gas, coal, general, aviation, capital goods, transport oems - epm, chemicals, metals & mining, transport services, steel, agricultural commodities, transport oems, energy utilities & power generators, cement, food, beverage & tobacco, paper & forestry, real estate, construction 귀사 에서 수행한 에너지 관련 활동을 선택하십시오 . ` m7. environmental performance - climate change 활동 보고 연도에 귀 조직이 이 에너지 관련 활동을 수행했는지 여부를 표시하십시오 . 연료 소비량 ( 공급 원료 제외 ) 예 아니오 구매 또는 획득한 전기 소비량 구매 또는 획득한 열의 소비량 구매 또는 획득한 증기의 소비량 구매 또는 획득한 냉각 소비량 전기 , 열 , 증기 또는 냉각의 발생량 no change - 2026 source verified 7.30.1 indicate whether your organization undertook this energy-related activity in the reporting year single_select yes no change from last year no change question dependencies the energy-related activities that you select in response to 7.30 determine which energy breakdowns you will be prompted to respond to in the proceeding questions. please note, if your response to 7.30 is amended, data in dependent questions may be erased. requested content consumption of fuel (excluding feedstocks) (row 1) you should select ‘yes' in row 1 ‘consumption of fuel (excluding feedstocks)' if fuel was consumed inside your organizational boundary in the reporting year. all fuels accounted for in the calculation of scope 1 emissions (7.6) and fuels accounted for in the calculation of emissions from biogenic carbon (7.12.1) are included. consumption of nuclear fuel is not included. consumption of purchased or acquired electricity heat, steam and/or cooli... explanation of terms excluding feedstocks: fuels consumed as feedstocks are fuels that are not combusted for energy purposes. for example, naphtha and ethane are feedstocks that may be converted into petrochemical products such as ethylene, and should not be included. the steel industry is a special case because coke and fuel injectants consumed at the blast furnace serve as feedstocks and a source of energy. these fuels are considered feedstocks and should not be counted. however, all fuels consumed that are der... 귀사 에서 수행한 에너지 관련 활동을 선택하십시오 . ` 환경 성과 - questions.searchText: 7.30.1 report your organization's energy consumption totals (excluding feedstocks) in mwh. environmental performance - climate change matrix financial services, oil & gas, coal, general, aviation, capital goods, transport oems - questions.questionText: State how much fuel in MWh your organization has consumed (excluding feedstocks) by fuel type.
questions.questionKo: 귀사의 총 연료 소비량을 연료 유형별로 구분하여 입력하십시오. (원료용 연료 제외, 단위: MWh)

epm, chemicals, metals & mining, transport services, steel, agricultural commodities, transport oems, energy utilities & power generators, cement, food, beverage & tobacco, paper & forestry, real estate, construction 귀사가 연료 유형별로 소비한 연료 ( 공급 원료 제외 ) 의 양 (mwh) 을 기재하십시오 . ` m7. environmental performance - climate change 활동 발열량 전체 연료 소비 자가 전기 발전 자가 열 발전 자가 스팀 발전 자가 냉각 발전 자가 열병합 또는 삼중 발전 관련 설명 지속가능한 바이오매스 lhv( 낮은 발열량 ) hhv( 높은 발열량 ) 발열량 확인 불가 각 항목마다 최대 2,400 자로 작성 기타 바이오매스 기타 재생가능한 연료 ( 재생 수소 ) 석탄 석유 천연가스 기타 재생 불가능한 연료 ( 재생 불가능 수소 ) 전체 연료 소비량 modified question general guidance added that heating value should be consistent with heating value reported in 7.30.1. 7.30.7.1 total fuel mwh consumed by the organization numeric 7.30.7.2 mwh fuel consumed for self-generation of electricity numeric 7.30.7.3 mwh fuel consumed for self-generation of heat numeric 7.30.7.4 mwh fuel consumed for self-generation of steam numeric 7.30.7.5 mwh fuel consumed for self-generation of cooling numeric 7.30.7.6 mwh fuel consumed for self- cogeneration or self-trigeneration numeric 7.30.7.7 comment rich_text change from last year modified question question dependencies this question only appears if you select "consumption of fuel (excluding feedstock)" in 7.30. requested content general for each fuel application selected in 7.30.6 a column appears in the table in addition to the "mwh fuel consumed for self-generation of heat" and "total mwh consumed by the organization" columns. if no fuel application or only "consumption of fuel for the generation of heat" is selected in 7.30.6 then only the "total mwh consumed by the organization" column will appear. you should provide information for all fuel (excluding feedstocks) consumed by your organization in the reporting ye... explanation of terms biomass: any organic matter, i.e. biological material, available on a renewable basis. includes feedstock derived from animals or plants, such as wood and agricultural crops, and organic waste from municipal and industrial sources. biomass fuels should be sustainably sourced and certified where possible, and include: solid biofuels - solid fuels derived from biomass. includes feedstock derived from animals or plants, such as wood and agricultural crops, and organic waste from municipal and in... 귀사가 연료 유형별로 소비한 연료 ( 공급 원료 제외 ) 의 양 (mwh) 을 기재하십시오 . ` 환경 성과 - questions.searchText: 7.30.9 provide details on the electricity, heat, steam, and cooling your organization has generated and consumed in the reporting year. environmental performance - climate change matrix oil & gas, coal, general, aviation, capital goods, transport oems - (2) m7. environmental performance - climate change minor change new dropdown option "kosovo" and several minor edits to country/area names. 7.30.14.1 consumption of purchased electricity (mwh) numeric 7.30.14.2 consumption of self-generated electricity (mwh) numeric 7.30.14.4 consumption of purchased heat, steam, and cooling (mwh) numeric 7.30.14.5 consumption of self-generated heat, steam, and cooling (mwh) numeric 7.30.14.6 total electricity/heat/steam/cooling energy consumption (mwh) numeric change from last year minor change (2025 7.30.16) requested content country/area (column 0) organizations will see a row for each country/area they have selected in question 1.7. you should include consumption from both purchased/acquired energy and self-generated energy in this question. energy that is purchased but not physically consumed (e.g. traded power, financial instruments), or energy that is self-generated but not physically consumed, should not be included here. energy consumption figures should be for the reporting year only (as defined by your an... 7.7 에서 보고된 시장 기반 scope 2 수치에서 배출 계수가 0 또는 거의 0 에 가까운 전기 , 열 , 증기 및 / 또는 냉방량에 대한 세부 정보를 제공하십시오 . questions.questionText: Provide details on the electricity purchases that were accounted for at a zero or near-zero emission factor in the reporting year.
questions.questionKo: details on the electricity purchases that were accounted for at a zero 또는 near-zero emission factor in the 보고연도.을/를 입력하십시오.
questions.searchText: 7.30.15 provide details on the electricity purchases that were accounted for at a zero or near-zero emission factor in the reporting year. environmental performance - climate change matrix oil & gas, coal, general, aviation, capital goods, transport oems - epm, chemicals, metals & mining, transport services, steel, agricultural commodities, transport oems, cement, food, beverage & tobacco, paper & forestry, real estate, construction [국문 번역 검토 필요] provide details on the electricity purchases that were accounted for at a zero or near-zero emission factor in the reporting year. modified question question text updated so focus is on electricity only, not energy (heat/steam/cooling). question now appears based on reporting of any renewable or other low carbon energy purchases in 7.30.1. question now appears to re100 members. additional guidance specifies organizations can report third-party verification of claims in the absence of eacs in question 13.1.1. question now appears to financial services organizations that are re100 members. column header modified to clarify that focus is on electricity rather than energy and guidance modified to reflect this. new dropdown option "kosovo" and several minor edits to country/area names. dropdown options removed or modified to reflect question level changes. 'default delivered electricity from […] a grid that is 95% or more low-carbon…" now appears only for relevant countries. dropdown options removed or modified to reflect expansion to ocean. conditional logic fix - only appears to relevant organizations based on selection in column "sourcing method". new column - only appears for re100 organizations and appears based on selection in technology type column. column header updated to reflect changes to question. conditional logic fix - only appears to relevant organizations based on selection in column "sourcing method". 7.30.15.1 country/area of questions.questionText: Provide details on the average emission factor used for all transport movements per mode that directly source energy from the grid.

questions.searchText: 7.30.16 provide details on the average emission factor used for all transport movements per mode that directly source energy from the grid. environmental performance - climate change matrix aviation, transport services 보고연도의 국가/지역별 전력/열/스팀/냉방 소비량 세부내역을 입력하십시오. no change - no change - 2026 source verified change from last year no change requested content general select "yes" if your organization consumes any types of fuels - fossil fuels (e.g. oil or natural gas) or renewable sources such as biomass - as feedstocks for the production of chemicals, regardless of whether the feedstock is purchased or produced by the organization. explanation of terms feedstocks : feedstocks are starting materials, ranging from fossil fuels to biomass-based resources. these materials are fed into a process, and converted into other commodities or resources, which are either used directly or further transformed. for example, in the steel industry, coking coal is converted to coke, which is used in the steel production. in the petrochemical industry, gaseous feedstocks (ethane, propane, or butane) are used to produce high value chemicals. 귀사는 consume fuels as feedstocks for chemical production activities입니까? requested that you provide this figure. however, this is not necessarily alwa... example response worked example of calculating emissions intensities figures a reporting organization has gross total combined scope 1 and 2 emissions of 300,000 metric tons co2e, revenue of 5 million us$ and 3,000 fte employees. in this case, the company could calculate and report its emission intensity figures by revenue and by fte as follows: 1. emissions intensity in metric tons co2e per unit currency total revenue intensity = 300,000 (metric tons co2e)/5,000,000 (us$)= 0.06 2. emissions intensity in metr... explanation of terms intensity metrics: intensity metrics describe an organization's co2e emissions in the context of another business metric. in this way, the emissions are normalized to account for growth. intensity is calculated by dividing the co2e emissions figure (the numerator) by an alternative business metric (the denominator), such as the number of full-time equivalent employees, the revenue or tons of aggregate produced. revenue: gross income arising from the operations of an organization over a period... 보고 연도의 전 세계 총 scope 1 및 2 배출량을 단위 통화 총 매출당 미터톤 co2e 로 설명하고 비즈니스 운영에 적합한 추가 questions.questionText: For your electric utility activities, provide a breakdown of your Scope 1 emissions and emissions intensity relating to your total power plant capacity and generation during the reporting year by source.
questions.questionKo: 전력 유틸리티 활동에 대해 총 발전소 용량 및 발전량 관련 Scope 1 배출량과 배출집약도를 세분화하여 입력하십시오.
questions.searchText: 7.46 for your electric utility activities, provide a breakdown of your scope 1 emissions and emissions intensity relating to your total power plant capacity and generation during the reporting year by source. environmental performance - climate change matrix energy utilities & power generators [국문 번역 검토 필요] for your electric utility activities, provide a breakdown of your scope 1 emissions and emissions intensity relating to your total power plant capacity and generation during the reporting year by source. modified question power generation sources updated to align with 1.16.1. additional guidance for specific power generation sources added. conditional logic fix - column now shows if "yes" is selected in column 1 "own or control…" of 1.16.1 conditional logic fix - column now shows if "gross electricity generation gwh" in 1.16.1 is greater than 0 conditional logic fix - column now shows if "gross" is selected in column 2 conditional logic fix - column now shows if "net" is selected in column 2 7.46.1 absolute scope 1 emissions (metric tons co2e) numeric 7.46.2 emissions intensity based on gross or net electricity generation single_select gross net 7.46.3 scope 1 emissions intensity (gross generation) numeric 7.46.4 scope 1 emissions intensity (net generation) numeric change from last year modified question question dependencies this question only appears if you select "electricity generation" in response to question 1.16. requested content general rows will appear in this question where "yes" was selected in column 1 "power generation source" of 1.16.1 report absolute scope 1 emissions and scope 1 emissions intensity for the primary power generation sources owned or controlled by the company, as reported in 1.16.1. gross electricity generation is the total amount of electric energy produced by generating units and measured at the generating terminal. net electricity generation is the amount of gross generation less the electric... explanation of terms renewable energy: energy taken from sources that are inexhaustible, e.g. wind, water, solar, geothermal energy and sustainable biofuels (adapted from the ghg protocol, 2004 ). biomass: any organic matter, i.e. biological material, available on a renewable basis. includes feedstock derived from animals or plants, such as wood and agricultural crops, and organic waste from municipal and industrial sources. biomass fuels should be sustainably sourced and certified where possible, and include: so... for your electric utility activities, provide a breakdown of your scope 1 배출량 및 emissions intensity relating to your total power plant capacity 및 generation during the 보고연도 by source. 환경 성과 - average transport movements of your vehicles and the vehicles used in your supply chain. these are requested, normalized by the work done (number of passengers or the amount of goods (mass) moved, as well as distance.). for each of the mode of transport applicable to your organization, please report your most relevant intensity metric. for freight transport, this means an intensity metric in tco2e per m... additional information global logistics emissions council framework: for the guidance on calculating these metrics, you may wish to consider the glec ( global logistics emissions council ) framework for logistics emissions accounting. this is a global framework that seeks to combine many existing standards and present a unified and globally comparable metric for logistics emissions accounting. many concepts and recommendations in this guidance have been based on the glec measurement framework. companies who have al... explanation of terms carbon efficiency of transportation: the carbon efficiency of transportation, as defined by the glec framework, is the amount of carbon emitted by a carrier to complete a combination of tasks, e.g. transporting goods for one or more customers as efficiently as possible. glec framework: the global logistics emissions council (glec) framework is a universal and transparent way of calculating logistics emissions across the global questions.questionText: Did you have an emissions target that was active in the reporting year?
questions.questionKo: 보고연도에 진행 중인(active) 온실가스 감축목표가 있습니까?
questions.reviewStatus: 변경 없음 확인 및 2026 원문 반영
questions.searchText: 7.53 did you have an emissions target that was active in the reporting year? environmental performance - climate change multi_select financial services, oil & gas, coal, general, aviation, capital goods, transport oems - epm, chemicals, metals & mining, transport services, steel, agricultural commodities, transport oems, energy utilities & power generators, cement, food, beverage & tobacco, paper & forestry, real estate, construction 보고 연도에 활동한 배출량 목표가 있습니까 ? m7. environmental performance - climate change 절대 목표 원단위 목표 포트폴리오 목표 [fs only] 목표 없음 no change - 2026 source verified change from last year no change requested content general targets that are based on a future "business as usual" year are not equivalent to emissions reduction targets and therefore should not be reported here. acceptable targets must determine emissions reductions through comparison to a set base year in the past, not to a projected "business as usual" emissions figure in the future. you have an "active target" if the target ends in or after the reporting year and the target is to reduce absolute emissions or emissions intensity. absolute t... additional information examples of emissions reduction targets the following are examples of absolute targets: metric tons co 2 e or % reduction from base year metric tons co 2 e or % reduction in product use phase relative to base year metric tons co 2 e or % reduction in supply chain relative to base year metric tons co 2 e or % reduction per year metric tons co 2 e or % reduction relative to 5 year rolling average of emissions cap on emissions in metric co 2 e the following are examples of intensity targets: met... 보고 연도에 활동한 배출량 목표가 있습니까 ? content general note that cdp is requesting data on gross emissions targets. gross means total emissions before any deductions or other adjustments are made to take account of offset credits, avoided emissions, and/or reductions attributable to the sequestration or transfer of ghgs (except in a specific case of bioenergy use for science-based targets and sbti-approved flag targets, which include both emissions and removals from land - see "additional information" for more details). if you have a targ... additional information science-based targets nearly 200 nations at cop21 wrote into the paris agreement that globally we will aim to limit warming to below 2°c and pursue efforts to limit warming to under 1.5°c. however, there is a large gap between the level of ambition of the country/area commitments and targeted temperatures. companies, which are responsible for a vast majority of the world's emissions, must play a critical role in filling the gap left by country/area commitments by raising the level of ambition... ambition organizations make progress against emissions targets that reflect their full emissions inventory and are in line with the science based targets initiative (sbti) criteria. example response worked example of absolute target table the following table shows two absolute target examples: a target to reduce covered scope 1 and 2 emissions by 80% in 2026 compared with the base year (id=abs1); a target to reduce covered scope 3 emissions by 75% in 2027 compared with the base year (id=abs2); target reference number is this a science-based target? science based targets initiative official validation letter target ambition date target was set target coverage greenhouse gases covered by t... explanation of terms maintenance target: a target to maintain a certain level of performance, such as the level of emissions reductions achieved after meeting a near-term target (e.g., a target to maintain a 90% reduction in scope 1+2 emissions compared to the base year). sectoral decarbonization approach: a sectoral decarbonization approach is a methodology for companies to establish greenhouse gas (ghg) reduction targets by allocating a sector-specific carbon budget based on subsector-level considerations. the ... requested content - continued base year emissions covered by target in all selected scopes as % of total base year emissions in all selected scopes (column 53) enter the total base year emissions covered by the target (reported in column 32) as a percentage of your total organization-wide base year emissions in all scopes selected in column 8 "scopes". if the target encompasses multiple scopes, note that you should not sum the percentages reported in 33, 34 and/or 52. e.g. if your target relates this question only appears if you select "intensity target" in response to 7.53. requested content general note that cdp is requesting data on gross emissions targets. gross means total emissions before any deductions or other adjustments are made to take account of offset credits, avoided emissions, and/or reductions attributable to the sequestration or transfer of ghgs (except in a specific case of bioenergy use for science-based targets - see "additional information" for more details). if you have a target that will be met in part by offsetting (including carbon neutrality targets), or ... additional information science-based targets nearly 200 nations at cop21 wrote into the paris agreement that globally we will aim to limit warming to below 2°c and pursue efforts to limit warming to under 1.5°c. however, there is a large gap between the level of ambition of the country/area commitments and targeted temperatures. companies, which are responsible for a vast majority of the world's emissions, must play a critical role in filling the gap left by country/area commitments by raising the level of ambition... ambition organizations make progress against emissions targets that reflect their full emissions inventory and are in line with the science based targets initiative (sbti) criteria. example response the table below shows two intensity target examples: int 1: a target to reduce total emissions (scope 1 and market-based scope 2) per us$ revenue from company-wide operations by 40% by 2025, from a base year of 2019. the emissions in the base year are 0.0005 tco2e/usd. the target emission intensity reduction would then be 0.0005 x 0.6 = 0.0003 tco2e/usd. the target was set in 2020. int 2: a target to reduce emissions from business flights per fte employee by 50% by 2026, from a base year of 2... explanation of terms maintenance target : a target to maintain a certain level of performance, such as the level of emissions reductions achieved after meeting a near-term target (e.g., a target to maintain a 90% reduction in scope 1+2 emissions compared to the base year). sectoral decarbonization approach (sda): a sectoral decarbonization approach is a methodology for companies to establish greenhouse gas (ghg) reduction targets by allocating a sector-specific carbon budget based on subsector-level consideration... requested content - continued % of total base year emissions in scope 3 (in all scope 3 categories) covered by this scope 3 intensity figure (column 53) this column only appears if you select "scope 3" in column 8 "scopes". enter the base year scope 3 emissions covered by the target as a percentage of your total organization-wide base year emissions for all scope 3 categories calculated in the base year. e.g. if you have selected effective environmental disclosures is important at every stage of the journey. there are a number of methodologies organizations in the financial sector can use to set and/or communicate portfolio achieve net zero by 2050. explanation of terms year-on-year rolling target: a target to achieve a certain level of performance every year (e.g., a target to reduce scope 1 emissions by 5% compared to ` m7. environmental performance - climate change 저탄소 에너지 소비 또는 생산량 증가 또는 유지 목표 메탄 배출량 감축 목표 넷제로 목표 기타 기후 관련 목표 기타 기후 관련 목표 없음 additional guidance added guidance for oil & gas and coal sectors on where to report flaring reduction targets. change from last year additional guidance requested content note for oil and gas and coal sectors: if you have a methane-specific emissions reduction target that was not reported in 7.53.1/7.53.2, select "targets to reduce methane emissions". you will then receive a follow up question 7.54.2 where you can provide details of your methane-specific emissions reduction target. if you engage in oil and gas or coal mining activities and have not selected "targets to reduce methane emissions" in this question, you will receive a follow up question 7.54.4 req... explanation of terms net-zero target: the sbti net-zero standard defines corporate net-zero as: reducing scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions to zero or to a residual level that is consistent with reaching net-zero emissions at the global or sector level in eligible 1.5°c scenarios or sector pathways and; neutralizing any residual emissions at the net-zero target date and any ghg emissions released into the atmosphere thereafter. target to reduce methane emissions , or "methane-specific target" is any target to reduce spec... 보고 연도에 활동한 다른 기후 관련 목표가 있습니까 ? low-carbon energy consumption or production" in response to 7.54. requested content general if you are a member of the re100 initiative, you can use this question to self-report your progress towards achieving your re100 target. note that re100 will use the data you report in other sections of this module (energy-related activities, electricity transmission and distribution) to come to its own assessment of your progress towards your re100 target. if you have interim targets, they can be reported in this question in additional rows. if you have a renewable electricity procur... example response the table below shows three low-carbon energy target examples: 1. low 1: a company-wide re100 target to increase the proportion of electricity consumed from renewable sources from 30% to 100% within 10 years from 2015. this target is part of the company's absolute scope 2 emissions reduction target reported in c4.1a. 2. low 2: a company-wide target to increase the proportion of heat consumed from low-carbon sources by 2% per year. this is a year-on-year rolling target that was set in 2010, th... explanation of terms low-carbon energy : in line with the iea definition, low-carbon technologies are technologies that produce low - or zero - greenhouse-gas emissions while operating. in the power sector this includes fossil-fuel plants fitted with carbon capture and storage, nuclear plants and renewable-based generation technologies. natural gas, combined cycle gas turbine and fossil fuel-based combined heat and power (cogeneration), despite being less carbon intensive than other means of electricity productio... 저탄소 에너지 소비 또는 생산을 늘리거나 유지하기 위한 목표에 대한 세부 정보를 제공하십시오 . ep100 and/or ev100 initiative, you can use this question to report o... explanation of terms maintenance target : a target to maintain a certain level of performance, such as the level of emissions reductions achieved after meeting a near-term target (e.g., a target to maintain a 90% reduction in scope 1+2 emissions compared to the base year). year-on-year rolling target : a target to achieve a certain level of performance every year (e.g., a target to reduce scope 1 emissions by 5% compared to the previous year). 메탄 감축 목표를 포함한 기타 기후 관련 목표에 대한 세부 정보를 제공하십시오 . based targets initiative has developed a standard for corporate net-zero targets, to ensure that companies' net-zero targets translate into action that is consistent with achieving a net-zero world by no later than 2050. ambition companies make progress against net-zero targets that are in line with the science based targets initiative (sbti) criteria. explanation of terms beyond value chain mitigation: mitigation action or investments that fall outside of a company's value chain. this includes activities that avoid or reduce greenhouse gas emissions, and those that remove and store questions.questionText: Identify the total number of initiatives at each stage of development, and for those in the implementation stages, the estimated CO2e savings.
questions.questionKo: 각 진행단계별 감축활동의 수를 입력하십시오. 만약, 감축활동이 이행단계에 있을 경우, 예상 감축량을 입력하십시오.
questions.searchText: 7.55.1 identify the total number of initiatives at each stage of development, and for those in the implementation stages, the estimated co2e savings. environmental performance - climate change matrix financial services, oil & gas, coal, general, aviation, capital goods, transport oems - epm, chemicals, metals & mining, transport services, steel, agricultural commodities, transport oems, energy utilities & power generators, cement, food, beverage & tobacco, paper & forestry, real estate, construction 각 개발 단계의 총 이니셔티브 수와 구현 단계에 있는 계획의 예상 co2e 절감량을 확인하십시오 . 이 질문은 m7.55 에 대한 응답으로 " 예 " 를 선택한 경우에만 나타납니다 . ` m7. environmental performance - climate change 진행 단계 이니셔티브 수 연간 총 온실가스 예상 감축량 ( 별표 (*) 로 표시된 행만 ) 검토중 이행예정* 이행중 * 이행완료* 이행되지 않음 revised question dependency guidance added to specify only initiatives to reduce emissions in the organization's value chain are relevant and question dependency updated to reflect the changes to 7.55. 7.55.1.1 number of initiatives numeric 7.55.1.2 total estimated annual co2e savings in metric tonnes co2e numeric change from last year revised question dependency question dependencies this question only appears if you select "yes" in response to column 1 "emissions reduction initiatives that were active within the reporting year" in 7.55 requested content general only initiatives to reduce co2e emissions within your organization's value chain (i.e. scope 1, scope 2 and/or scope 3) should be reported here. stage of development (column 1) report the initiatives in the following stages of development: under investigation: a potential initiative to reduce emissions that is being evaluated but not yet approved by your company during the reporting year. to be implemented: an initiative to reduce emissions that has been approved for implementation by... 각 개발 단계의 총 이니셔티브 수와 구현 단계에 있는 계획의 예상 co2e 절감량을 확인하십시오 . 이 질문은 환경 성과 - any emissions reduction initiatives made. there is no need to record every action - initiatives can be recorded on a programmatic level. companies with large numbers of initiatives should prioritize those that have the potential to provide a meaningful contribution to emissions reductions. it is acknowledged that maintenance activities can have a beneficial impact on carbon emissions. only those questions.questionText: What methods do you use to drive investment in emissions reduction activities?
questions.questionKo: 배출량 감축활동에 대한 투자를 유도하기 위해 사용한 방법을 입력하십시오.
questions.searchText: 7.55.3 what methods do you use to drive investment in emissions reduction activities? environmental performance - climate change matrix financial services, oil & gas, coal, general, aviation, capital goods, transport oems - epm, chemicals, metals & mining, transport services, steel, agricultural commodities, transport oems, energy utilities & power generators, cement, food, beverage & tobacco, paper & forestry, real estate, construction 배출량 감축활동 투자를 이끌어내기 위해 사용한 방법을 입력해 주십시오 . 이 질문은 m7.55 에 대한 응답으로 " 예 " 를 선택한 경우에만 나타납니다 . ` m7. environmental performance - climate change 방법 관련 설명 규제 요건 / 표준 준수 에너지 효율을 위한 전용 예산 저탄소 제품 r&d 를 위한 전용 예산 기타 배출량 감축 활동을 위한 전용 예산 직원 참여 재무 최적화 계산 탄소 내부 가격 내부 인센티브 / 인정 프로그램 내부 금융 메커니즘 낮은 투자 수익률 (roi) 사양 한계 감축 비용 곡선 기술 개발을 위한 정부와의 파트너십 기타 최대 1,500 자로 작성 revised question dependency added guidance to specify only initiatives to reduce emissions in the organization's value chain are relevant. 7.55.3.1 method single_select compliance with regulatory requirements/standards dedicated budget for energy efficiency dedicated budget for low-carbon product r&d dedicated budget for other emissions reduction activities employee engagement financial optimization calculations internal price on carbon internal incentives/recognition programs internal finance mechanisms lower return on investment (roi) specification marginal abatement cost curve partnering with governments on technology development other 7.55.3.2 comment rich_text change from last year revised question dependency question dependencies this question only appears if you select "yes" in response to column 1 "emissions reduction initiatives that were active within the reporting year" in 7.55 requested content general this question is intended to gather information on the ways in which capital is directed towards emissions reduction activities within your organization's value chain (i.e. scope 1, scope 2 and/or scope 3), and/or the way in which initiatives are identified. if your organization uses an internal carbon price you are encouraged to report this here in addition to in module 5 "business strategy" section "pricing environmental externalities". method (column 1) select the types of methods ... additional information marginal abatement cost curves marginal abatement cost curves, or maccs, provide a method of evaluating potential emissions reduction activities. they provide a visual comparison of the marginal abatement costs for different projects. maccs can be generated to evaluate options at any level of organization - climate change matrix oil & gas [국문 번역 검토 필요] if flaring is relevant to your oil and gas production and processing activities, describe your organization's efforts to reduce flaring, including any flaring reduction targets. modified question new column 7.62.1 occurrence of flaring in production and/or processing activities single_select yes, both non-emergency and emergency flaring yes, only emergency flaring no, we do not use flaring 7.62.2 flaring reduction targets single_select flaring target reported in 7.54.2 no flaring targets have been set 7.62.3 efforts to reduce flaring multi_select improvements in efficiency gas conservation and reinjection upgrading equipment other, please specify no efforts to reduce flaring 7.62.4 involvement in voluntary flaring reduction programs multi_select world bank zero routine flaring by 2030 national program, please specify other, please specify no involvement 7.62.5 primary reason for not setting flaring reduction targets single_select evaluation in progress important but not an immediate business priority judged to be unimportant, explanation provided lack of internal resources insufficient data on operations no instruction from management other, please specify 7.62.6 please explain rich_text change from last year modified question requested content general flaring can occur in the coal industry for many reasons, ranging from initial start-up testing of a facility to unplanned equipment malfunctions. flaring includes emissions of co2, ch4, and n2o from elevated flares, ground flares, emergency flares, well-testing and well work-over. please explain (column 6) if you select "no, we do not use flaring" or "yes, only emergency flaring" in column 1, give further details confirming no use of non-emergency (also known as ‘routine') flaring in ... flaring is relevant to your oil 및 gas production 및 프로세스ing activities, 설명 your organization's efforts to reduce flaring인 경우, including any flaring reduction 목표. 환경 성과 - questions.searchText: 7.67 do you implement agriculture or forest management practices on your own land with a climate change mitigation and/or adaptation benefit? environmental performance - climate change single_select agricultural commodities, food, beverage & tobacco, paper & forestry [국문 번역 검토 필요] do you implement agriculture or forest management practices on your own land with a climate change mitigation and/or adaptation benefit? no question-level change; detail changes definitions of "climate change adaptation" and "climate change mitigation" updated to align with cdp glossary definitions. change from last year no change question dependencies this question only appears if you select "own land only" or "value chain (including own land)" column "relevance of emissions and/or water-related impacts" for "production" row of 1.11. requested content general select ‘yes' if you have implemented/are in the process of implementing actions on your land with direct or indirect climate change benefits. these land management actions may have been adopted for either preventing, reducing, controlling, and/or adapting to effects of climate change. there is a wide variety of agricultural/forestry management practices that have either direct or indirect climate change mitigation and/or adaptation benefits. explanation of terms adaptation : in human systems, the process of adjustment to actual or expected climate and its effects, in order to moderate harm or exploit beneficial opportunities. in natural systems, the process of adjustment to actual climate and its effects; human intervention may facilitate adjustment to expected climate and its effects ( ipcc , 2018 ). climate change mitigation: the process of holding the increase in the global average temperature to well below 2°c and pursuing efforts to limit it to ... 귀사는 implement agriculture 또는 forest management practices on your own land with a climate change mitigation 및/또는 adaptation benefit입니까? questions.questionText: Does your organization assess the life cycle emissions of any of its products or services?
questions.questionKo: 귀사는 assess the life cycle emissions of any of its products 또는 services입니까?
questions.searchText: 7.71 does your organization assess the life cycle emissions of any of its products or services? environmental performance - climate change matrix capital goods [국문 번역 검토 필요] does your organization assess the life cycle emissions of any of its products or services? no change - 2026 source verified 7.71.1 assessment of life cycle emissions single_select yes no, but we plan to start doing so within the next two years no, and we do not plan to start doing so within the next two years 7.71.2 comment rich_text change from last year no change requested content general select "yes" if you assess the life cycle emissions of any of your products or services. you will then be requested to provide further details in the following question. comment (column 2) (optional) if you do not assess life cycle emissions, you may wish to use this column to explain why not and/or explain your plan to start doing so in the future. additional information life cycle assessment (lca ): a structured, comprehensive method of quantifying material- and energy-flows and their associated emissions in the life cycles of products (i.e. goods and services). emissions assessments are a component of an lca, but full lcas cover all environmental impacts of a studied product. relationship between the life cycle emissions of products and an organization's ghg emissions inventory: the assessment of product life cycle emissions and of scope 3 emissions serves ... explanation of terms life cycle : consecutive and interlinked stages of a product system, from the acquisition of raw materials or generation of natural resources to end-of-life. life cycle emissions: ghg emissions from a product or service throughout its life cycle. 귀사는 assess the life cycle emissions of any of its products 또는 services입니까? questions.searchText: 7.74 do you classify any of your existing goods and/or services as low-carbon products? environmental performance - climate change single_select oil & gas, coal, general, aviation, capital goods, transport oems - epm, chemicals, metals & mining, transport services, steel, agricultural commodities, transport oems, energy utilities & power generators, cement, food, beverage & tobacco, paper & forestry, real estate, construction 귀사의 기존 상품 및 / 또는 서비스 중 저탄소 제품으로 분류되는 것이 있습니까 ? ` m7. environmental performance - climate change 예 아니오 no change - 2026 source verified change from last year no change requested content general low-carbon products and/or services are important to aid the transition to a net-zero carbon economy and to ensure that global average temperature increase above pre-industrial level stays below 1.5°c. while there are various low-carbon product/service taxonomies and definitions, cdp broadly defines them as products or services which have comparatively lower emissions across their entire life cycle (i.e. from material acquisition through to product end-of-life) when compared to a base... additional information how do you define a low-carbon product? despite the increasing focus from investors on low-carbon products, there remains a level of ambiguity over the definition of what constitutes a ‘low-carbon product'. instead, there has been a greater focus on the benefits of their creation and use, one of which is aiding in the transition towards a net-zero carbon economy operating within the limits set out by leading climate scientists to ensure that global average temperature increase above pre-indus... explanation of terms baseline scenario: a reference case that represents the events or conditions most likely to occur in the absence of the low-carbon product in the consequential approach to estimating avoided emissions. reference product: the product against which the low-carbon product is compared in the attributional approach to estimating avoided emissions. attributional approach: the most commonly used approach at present to estimate avoided emissions - measures the difference in total life-cycle ghg emiss... 귀사의 기존 상품 및 / 또는 서비스 중 저탄소 제품으로 분류되는 것이 있습니까 ? 7.74. requested content level of aggregation (column 1) select from the drop-down menu what level of aggregation you wish to report on in this row. for example, you may only produce one product that can be classified as ‘low carbon.' in this case you may want to report at the product level of aggregation. alternatively, if your company produces several low carbon products that have a similar function, you may wish to report at the "group of products or services" level. note that you can add multiple rows to this tab... additional information how do you define a low-carbon product? despite the increasing focus from investors on low-carbon products, there remains a level of ambiguity over the definition of what constitutes a ‘low-carbon product'. instead, there has been a greater focus on the benefits of their creation and use, one of which is aiding in the transition towards a net-zero carbon economy operating within the limits set out by leading climate scientists to ensure that global average temperature increase above pre-indus... example response worked examples of low-carbon products example 1: company a is a paper production company. it has a range of products that can be classified as low-carbon as these products are made from recycled material so have comparatively lower emissions than paper made from virgin material. level of aggregation taxonomy used to classify product(s) or service(s) as low-carbon type of product(s) or service(s) description of product(s) or service(s) have you estimated the avoided emissions of this low-carb... explanation of terms baseline scenario: a reference case that represents the events or conditions most likely to occur in the absence of the low-carbon product in the consequential approach to estimating avoided emissions. reference product: the product against which the low-carbon product is compared in the attributional approach to estimating avoided emissions. gate-to-gate: the emissions and removals questions.searchText: 7.76 does your organization manage net zero carbon buildings? environmental performance - climate change single_select real estate [국문 번역 검토 필요] does your organization manage net zero carbon buildings? no change - 2026 source verified change from last year no change question dependencies this question only appears if you select "buildings management" in response to 1.15. requested content general indicate whether your organization has net zero carbon buildings under management. if you respond "yes", you will be requested to provide details on your net zero carbon buildings in the following question. if you respond "no", you will be requested to explain your organization's plan with regards to managing net zero carbon buildings in the future, or explain why you do not plan to do so. explanation of terms net zero carbon building : in line with the world green building council's definition, a building that is highly energy efficient and fully powered from on-site and/or off-site renewable energy sources. more specific definitions have been developed by some local green building councils as well as in some national regulations. if you are operating in countries/areas where the definition of net zero carbon building has been defined in a legal document (e.g. building regulations and energy decre... 귀사는 manage net zero carbon buildings입니까? 2026 source verified change from last year no change question dependencies this question only appears if you select "no, but we plan to in the future" or "no, and we do not plan to in the future" in response to 7.76 or 7.77. requested content general explain your organization's plan, including time-frame: to include net zero carbon buildings in your portfolio (for organizations involved in real estate management); or to start developing or constructing net zero carbon buildings (for real estate developers and construction companies); if you are not planning to start transitioning to net zero carbon buildings, explain why not. explain your organization's plan to manage, develop 또는 construct net zero carbon buildings, 또는 그 이유를 설명 you do not plan to do so. 환경 성과 - questions.questionText: Has your organization retired any project-based carbon credits within the reporting year?
questions.questionKo: 보고연도 내에 프로젝트 기반 탄소크레딧을 사용했습니까?
questions.reviewStatus: 변경 없음 확인 및 2026 원문 반영
questions.searchText: 7.79 has your organization retired any project-based carbon credits within the reporting year? environmental performance - climate change single_select financial services, oil & gas, coal, general, aviation, capital goods, transport oems - epm, chemicals, metals & mining, transport services, steel, agricultural commodities, transport oems, energy utilities & power generators, cement, food, beverage & tobacco, paper & forestry, real estate, construction 보고연도 내에 프로젝트 기반 탄소 크레딧을 사용했습니까 ? ` m7. environmental performance - climate change 예 아니오 no change - 2026 source verified change from last year no change requested content general "retiring" a credit means that the emissions reductions or removals associated with the credit have been claimed for compliance requirements or voluntary goals and cannot be used again, and the exact term used may vary, e.g. retired, surrendered, claimed or used. for further information, please check the technical note " retirement vs. cancellation of instruments ." select "yes" if you have retired credits during the reporting period, regardless of when you have acquired them. select ... ambition companies prioritize emissions reductions in their value chain, and only use high-quality carbon credits to neutralize the impact of sources of residual emissions that cannot be eliminated through value-chain emissions reductions. explanation of terms cancellation : the permanent removal of a carbon credit in an electronic registry without claiming the associated emission reductions or removals towards any voluntary or mandatory targets or other purposes. cancellation may include the following purposes: compensating for reversals; compensation for any previous excess issuance; administrative cancellation for the purpose of re-issuing carbon credits for the same emission reductions or removals under a different carbon-crediting program. onl... 보고연도 내에 프로젝트 기반 탄소 크레딧을 사용했습니까 ? ` 환경 성과 - 기후변화 단일 선택 climate change 금융 서비스, 석유 및 가스, 석탄, 일반, 항공, 자본재, 운송 oem - epm, 화학, 금속 및 광업, 운송 서비스, 철강, 농산물 원자재, 운송 oem, 에너지 유틸리티 및 발전, 시멘트, food, beverage & tobacco, 제지 및 임업, 부동산, 건설 보고연도 내에 프로젝트 기반 탄소크레딧을 사용했습니까? questions.searchText: 7.79.1 provide details of the project-based carbon credits retired by your organization in the reporting year. environmental performance - climate change matrix financial services, oil & gas, coal, general, aviation, capital goods, transport oems - epm, chemicals, metals & mining, transport services, steel, agricultural commodities, transport oems, energy utilities & power generators, cement, food, beverage & tobacco, paper & forestry, real estate, construction 보고연도에 사용한(canceled) 프로젝트 기반 탄소크레딧에 대한 세부내용을 입력하십시오. minor change dropdown option 'ers - ecosystem restoration standard' repalced with 'equitable earth (formerly ers). 7.79.1.1 project type single_select afforestation agriculture agroforestry biochar bioenergy with carbon capture and storage (beccs) biomass energy cement coal mine/bed methane clean cookstove distribution community projects direct air capture (dac) energy distribution energy efficiency: households energy efficiency: …, please specify" and provide the relevant data point. note: you cannot select multiple modules in a row - you should add a new row for each module in... additional information for further details on good practice for verification and assurance refer to cdp's guide on the business benefits of third-party verification of environmental data . there is no requirement for data to be verified twice, as such data that has already been verified for one scheme/reporting effort can normally be used for another. check the specifications of your verification body/evidence to ensure this is the case. ambition organizations disclose and provide evidence that the environmental data within their cdp response has been verified, through credible standards. explanation of terms certification : the
